content: For families in the west of Ireland, access to safe, supported birth has never been straightforward. A standalone birth centre in Galway would change that — offering low-risk women a midwifery-led environment that is neither home nor a busy obstetric unit. The evidence from the UK Birthplace cohort study and Cochrane reviews is clear: freestanding midwifery units are safe for low-risk women and associated with fewer interventions and higher satisfaction. Galway deserves the same choice available to families in Dublin, Cork, and Limerick.
excerpt: The case for a freestanding birth centre in Galway — evidence, access, and the families who need it most.
title: Why Galway Needs a Standalone Birth Centre

content: The day-5 postnatal visit is one of the most clinically significant contacts a community midwife makes. It is when feeding difficulties, signs of postnatal depression, and neonatal jaundice are most likely to present. In urban areas, that visit is manageable. In rural Connemara, it is a different story. A single midwife covering a 50km radius faces real logistical challenges that policy documents rarely acknowledge. This piece draws on my own caseload experience and the research I am conducting through my NUIG Masters to argue for a more honest conversation about rural postnatal staffing.
excerpt: What the day-5 visit really looks like in rural Connemara — and why staffing policy needs to catch up.
title: Day-5 Postnatal Visits: A Rural Midwife's Perspective

content: If you have never run the Sky Road loop out of Clifden, put it on your list. Starting from the town centre and climbing west past Streamstown Bay, the full loop covers just over 12km with around 200m of elevation. The views on a clear day are extraordinary — out to Clifden Bay, across to Inishbofin, and the bog stretching inland in every direction. I run it most weekends when I am staying at Hampton's Rest and it never gets old. Tips: park in Clifden, bring waterproofs regardless of the forecast, and do the loop clockwise for the best descent views.
excerpt: A personal guide to the Sky Road loop from a midwife who runs it most weekends.
title: Running the Sky Road: Connemara's Most Beautiful Route

content: Hampton's Rest B&B sits at the beginning of the Sky Road, two minutes from Clifden town centre and a world away from the noise of the week. We have three rooms, a full Irish breakfast using local produce from the Connemara region, and genuine Galway hospitality. If you are visiting for the Connemara Marathon, a walking holiday, or simply passing through on a west of Ireland road trip, come and stay. Joan and the family will make sure you leave well rested and well fed. Bookings via email at stay@hamptonsrest.ie or call directly.
excerpt: About Hampton's Rest B&B on the Sky Road, Clifden — a personal welcome from the Hampton family.
title: Hampton's Rest: Where Clifden Meets the Sky
